You can find the latest list of models supported by OpenRouter [here](https://openrouter.ai/models).\n\nYou can find the latest list of tool-supported models supported by OpenRouter [here](https://openrouter.ai/models?order=newest&supported_parameters=tools). (Note: This list may contain models that are not compatible with the AI SDK.)\n\n## Passing Extra Body to OpenRouter\n\nThere are 3 ways to pass extra body to OpenRouter:\n\n1. Via the `providerOptions.openrouter` property:\n\n   ```typescript\n   import { createOpenRouter } from '@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider';\n   import { streamText } from 'ai';\n\n   const openrouter = createOpenRouter({ apiKey: 'your-api-key' });\n   const model = openrouter('anthropic/claude-3.7-sonnet:thinking');\n   await streamText({\n     model,\n     messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello' }],\n     providerOptions: {\n       openrouter: {\n         reasoning: {\n           max_tokens: 10,\n         },\n       },\n     },\n   });\n   ```\n\n2. Via the `extraBody` property in the model settings:\n\n   ```typescript\n   import { createOpenRouter } from '@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider';\n   import { streamText } from 'ai';\n\n   const openrouter = createOpenRouter({ apiKey: 'your-api-key' });\n   const model = openrouter('anthropic/claude-3.7-sonnet:thinking', {\n     extraBody: {\n       reasoning: {\n         max_tokens: 10,\n       },\n     },\n   });\n   await streamText({\n     model,\n     messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello' }],\n   });\n   ```\n\n3. Via the `extraBody` property in the model factory.\n\n   ```typescript\n   import { createOpenRouter } from '@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider';\n   import { streamText } from 'ai';\n\n   const openrouter = createOpenRouter({\n     apiKey: 'your-api-key',\n     extraBody: {\n       reasoning: {\n         max_tokens: 10,\n       },\n     },\n   });\n   const model = openrouter('anthropic/claude-3.7-sonnet:thinking');\n   await streamText({\n     model,\n     messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello' }],\n   });\n   ```\n\n## Anthropic Prompt Caching\n\nYou can include Anthropic-specific options directly in your messages when using functions like `streamText`. The OpenRouter provider will automatically convert these messages to the correct format internally.\n\n### Basic Usage\n\n```typescript\nimport { createOpenRouter } from '@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider';\nimport { streamText } from 'ai';\n\nconst openrouter = createOpenRouter({ apiKey: 'your-api-key' });\nconst model = openrouter('anthropic/<supported-caching-model>');\n\nawait streamText({\n  model,\n  messages: [\n    {\n      role: 'system',\n      content:\n        'You are a podcast summary assistant. You are detail-oriented and critical about the content.',\n    },\n    {\n      role: 'user',\n      content: [\n        {\n          type: 'text',\n          text: 'Given the text body below:',\n        },\n        {\n          type: 'text',\n          text: `<LARGE BODY OF TEXT>`,\n          providerOptions: {\n            openrouter: {\n              cacheControl: { type: 'ephemeral' },\n            },\n          },\n        },\n        {\n          type: 'text',\n          text: 'List the speakers?',\n        },\n      ],\n    },\n  ],\n});\n```\n\n## Use Cases\n\n### Usage Accounting\n\nThe provider supports [OpenRouter usage accounting](https://openrouter.ai/docs/use-cases/usage-accounting), which allows you to track token usage details directly in your API responses, without making additional API calls.\n\n```typescript\n// Enable usage accounting\nconst model = openrouter('openai/gpt-3.5-turbo', {\n  usage: {\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

These hooks are framework agnostic, so they can be used in Next.js, React, Svelte, and Vue.\n\nYou need to install the package for your framework:\n\n```shell\nnpm install @ai-sdk/react\n```\n\n###### @/app/page.tsx (Next.js App Router)\n\n```tsx\n'use client';\n\nimport { useState } from 'react';\nimport { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react';\n\nexport default function Page() {\n  const { messages, status, sendMessage } = useChat();\n  const [input, setInput] = useState('');\n  const handleSubmit = e => {\n    e.preventDefault();\n    sendMessage({ text: input });\n    setInput('');\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <div>\n      {messages.map(message => (\n        <div key={message.id}>\n          <strong>{`${message.role}: `}</strong>\n          {message.parts.map((part, index) => {\n            switch (part.type) {\n              case 'text':\n                return <span key={index}>{part.text}</span>;\n\n              // other cases can handle images, tool calls, etc\n            }\n          })}\n        </div>\n      ))}\n\n      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>\n        <input\n          value={input}\n          placeholder=\"Send a message...\"\n          onChange={e => setInput(e.target.value)}\n          disabled={status !== 'ready'}\n        />\n      </form>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n```\n\n###### @/app/api/chat/route.ts (Next.js App Router)\n\n```ts\nimport { streamText } from 'ai';\nimport { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';\n\nexport async function POST(req: Request) {\n  const { messages } = await req.json();\n\n  const result = streamText({\n    model: openai('gpt-4o'),\n    system: 'You are a helpful assistant.',\n    messages,\n  });\n\n  return result.toUIMessageStreamResponse();\n}\n```\n\n## Templates\n\nWe've built [templates](https://vercel.com/templates?type=ai) that include AI SDK integrations for different use cases, providers, and frameworks. JSON.parse(item) : initialValue;\n    } catch (error) {\n      // If error also return initialValue\n      console.log(error);\n      return initialValue;\n    }\n  });\n\n  // Being repeatedly used in components that is being shown multiple times in a page makes this operation overlaps and causes innacurate updates.\n  // Return a wrapped version of useState's setter function that ...\n  // ... persists the new value to localStorage.\n  const setValue = (value: T | ((val: T) => T)) => {\n    try {\n      // Allow value to be a function so we have the same API as useState\n      const valueToStore =\n        value instanceof Function ? value(storedValue) : value;\n\n      // Save to local storage\n      if (typeof window !== \"undefined\") {\n        try {\n          const serializedValue = JSON.stringify(valueToStore);\n          // in order to fix the overlapping issue, we have\n\n          localStorage.setItem(key, serializedValue);\n          console.log(`✅ Successfully saved to localStorage: ${key}`);\n        } catch (storageError) {\n          if (\n            storageError instanceof DOMException &&\n            storageError.name === \"QuotaExceededError\"\n          ) {\n            console.error(`❌ localStorage quota exceeded for key: ${key}`);\n          } else {\n            console.error(\n              `❌ Failed to serialize/save to localStorage for key: ${key}`,\n              storageError\n            );\n          }\n          // Don't update state if localStorage save failed\n          return;\n        }\n      } else {\n        console.warn(\"⚠️ Cannot save to localStorage: running on server-side\");\n        // Still update state even if we can't persist\n      }\n\n      // Save state only after successful localStorage save (or on server-side)\n      setStoredValue(valueToStore);\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error(`❌ Unexpected error in setValue for key: ${key}`, error);\n    }\n  };\n\n  // this function will only updates and merge values given in the parameter to the correspondinng item inside the current localStorage, and thus preventing overlapping updates between components\n  const updateValue = <T>(value: T | ((val: T) => T)) => {\n    // Get from local storage by key\n    const currentLocalStorageData = localStorage.getItem(key);\n\n    if (currentLocalStorageData) {\n      try {\n        const parsedData = JSON.parse(currentLocalStorageData);\n        const newValue = value instanceof Function ? value(parsedData) : value;\n\n        // Merge the new value with the existing data\n        const updatedData = { ...parsedData, ...newValue };\n\n        // Save the updated data back to local storage\n        localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(updatedData));\n        setStoredValue(updatedData);\n      } catch (error) {\n        console.error(\n          `❌ Failed to update localStorage for key: ${key}`,\n          error\n        );\n      }\n    }\n  };\n\n  return [storedValue, setValue, updateValue];\n}\n","exit_code":2,"status":"failed"}}

boolean,\n    timeSpent: number\n  ) => {\n    setPracticePerformance((prevData) => {\n      const updatedData = { ...prevData };\n\n      // Create or update word performance\n      const wordPerf = wordPerf.totalAttempts;\n\n      // Update mastery level\n      wordPerf.masteryLevel = calculateMasteryLevel(\n        wordPerf.correctAttempts,\n        wordPerf.totalAttempts,\n        wordPerf.consecutiveCorrect\n      );\n\n      // Update word performance in data\n      updatedData.wordPerformance[word] = wordPerf;\n\n      // Create quiz attempt record\n      const attempt:      timeSpent,\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        difficulty: currentQuestion.word.difficulty,\n      };\n\n      // Add attempt to history\n      updatedData.attempts.push(attempt);\n\n      // Update overall statistics\n      updatedData.lastUpdated = Date.now();\n      const totalCorrect = updatedData.attempts.filter(\n        (a) => a.isCorrect\n      ).length;\n      updatedData.overallAccuracy = totalCorrect / updatedData.attempts.length;\n\n      // Categorize words based on performance\n      const allWords = Object.values(updatedData.wordPerformance);\n      updatedData.strongWords = allWords\n        .filter(\n          (w) =>\n            w.masteryLevel === \"mastered\" || w.masteryLevel === \"proficient\"\n        )\n        .map((w) => w.word);\n\n      updatedData.weakWords = allWords\n        .filter((w) => w.masteryLevel === \"struggling\")\n        .map((w) => w.word);\n\n      updatedData.improvingWords = allWords\n        .filter(\n          (w) => w.masteryLevel === \"learning\" && w.consecutiveCorrect > 0\n        )\n        .map((w) => w.word);\n\n      return updatedData;\n    });\n  };\n\n  const currentQuestion = quizQuestions[quizState.currentQuestionIndex];\n\n  const handleAnswerSelect = (answer: string) => {\n    playSound(\"tap-radio.wav\");\n    dispatch({ type: \"SELECT_ANSWER\", payload: answer });\n  };\n\n  const handleSubmitAnswer = () => {\n    if (!quizState.selectedAnswer) return;\n\n    playSound(\"button-pressed.wav\");\n    const isCorrect =\n      quizState.selectedAnswer === currentQuestion.correctAnswer;\n    const timeSpent = Math.round(\n      (Date.now() - quizState.questionStartTime) / 1000\n    ); // Convert to seconds\n\n    // Update quiz state\n    dispatch({ type: \"SUBMIT_ANSWER\", payload: { isCorrect } });\n\n    // Update practice performance data (only for new answers)\n    if (!quizState.answeredQuestions[quizState.currentQuestionIndex]) {\n      updateWordPerformance(currentQuestion.word.word, isCorrect, timeSpent);\n    }\n\n    if (isCorrect) {\n      playSound(\"correct-answer.wav\");\n    } else {\n      playSound(\"incorrect-answer.wav\");\n    }\n  };\n\n  const handleNextQuestion = () => {\n    playSound(\"button-pressed.wav\");\n\n    if (quizState.currentQuestionIndex + 1 >= quizQuestions.length) {\n      // Update total quizzes taken when completing a quiz\n      setPracticePerformance((prevData) => ({\n        ...prevData,\n        totalQuizzesTaken: prevData.totalQuizzesTaken + 1,\n        lastUpdated: Date.now(),\n      }));\n    }\n\n    dispatch({ type: \"NEXT_QUESTION\" });\n  };\n\n  const handlePreviousQuestion = () => {\n    playSound(\"button-pressed.wav\");\n    dispatch({ type: \"PREVIOUS_QUESTION\" });\n  };\n\n  const handleFinishPractice = () => {\n    playSound(\"button-pressed.wav\");\n\n    // Update total quizzes taken even if not completed\n
